intent: Check whether the candidate can define a Node.js module as a reusable code boundary with imports and exports, not just a file on disk.
primaryQuestion: What is a module in Node.js?
expectedSignals: Defines a module as a unit of JavaScript code that can expose values such as functions, classes, constants, or objects for other files to use.
Mentions that Node.js has built-in modules such as fs and path, third-party modules installed from npm, and local modules created in the project.
Explains that modules help organize code, avoid putting everything in one file, and create clearer boundaries between responsibilities.
Connects modules to importing and exporting through CommonJS require/module.exports or ES module import/export.
Understands that a module has its own scope, so top-level variables are not automatically global.
redFlags: Says a module is only an npm package.
Thinks variables in one file are automatically available in every other file.
Cannot explain import or export at a basic level.
Confuses Node.js modules with database modules or framework modules without linking back to JavaScript code reuse.

scoringHints.description: Clearly explains module scope, reuse, built-in versus third-party versus local modules, and how exports/imports connect files.
Gives a correct reusable-code definition but misses module scope or the different module sources.
Can say modules organize code but cannot explain how one module exposes code to another.
Answer shows no practical understanding of Node.js module boundaries.

followUps.question: Can you name one built-in module, one npm module, and one local module you might create in an API project?
followUps.purpose: Force the candidate to distinguish module sources concretely.

followUps.question: If you declare a variable at the top of a module file, is it automatically global in Node.js?

en.title: Node.js module basics
en.displayQuestion: What is a module in Node.js?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands modules as scoped, reusable code units.
guidance: Define a module as a code unit with explicit imports and exports.
Mention built-in, npm, and local modules.
Explain module scope and code organization benefits.
commonMistakes: Saying modules are only npm packages.
Assuming top-level variables are global.
Not explaining exports and imports.

vi.title: Nền tảng module trong Node.js
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu module là đơn vị code có scope riêng và có thể tái sử dụng hay không.
guidance: Định nghĩa module là đơn vị code có import và export rõ ràng.
Nhắc tới module built-in, module npm và module local.
Giải thích scope riêng của module và lợi ích tổ chức code.
commonMistakes: Nói module chỉ là npm package.
Tưởng biến top-level tự động là global.
Không giải thích được export và import.

ja.title: Node.js module の基礎
ja.displayQuestion: Node.js における module とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: module を scope を持つ再利用可能な code unit として理解しているか確認する。
guidance: module を明示的な import/export を持つ code unit として定義する。
built-in module、npm module、local module に触れる。
module scope と code organization の利点を説明する。
commonMistakes: module は npm package だけだと言う。
top-level 変数が自動的に global になると思う。
export と import を説明できない。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Modules - CommonJS
sourceReferences.note: Used for CommonJS module scope, require, and module.exports behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: ECMAScript modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for ES module support in Node.js.

intent: Check whether the candidate can compare CommonJS and ES Modules in Node.js at a practical junior level.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between CommonJS and ES Modules?
expectedSignals: Identifies CommonJS as the older Node.js module system using require and module.exports.
Identifies ES Modules as the standardized JavaScript module system using import and export.
Mentions that Node.js decides module mode using file extensions such as .cjs and .mjs or the package.json type field.
Explains that CommonJS loading is commonly synchronous, while ES Modules are statically analyzable and support features such as top-level await.
Mentions practical interop issues, such as ESM-only packages not always being require-able from CommonJS and default or named import differences.
redFlags: Says CommonJS and ES Modules are exactly the same except for syntax.
Ignores package.json type, .cjs, and .mjs when explaining how Node chooses module mode.
Claims require can import every ES module without constraints.
Cannot name the import/export keywords for ES Modules or require/module.exports for CommonJS.

scoringHints.description: Contrasts syntax, runtime mode selection, loading semantics, and interop risks in clear practical terms.
Knows the two systems and basic syntax but misses module mode detection or interop detail.
Recognizes the names but treats them as interchangeable snippets.
Answer would lead to broken imports in a real Node.js project.

followUps.question: What does the package.json type field change for .js files?
What might happen if a CommonJS app tries to require an ESM-only npm package?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands real interoperability failures.

en.title: CommonJS versus ES Modules
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between CommonJS and ES Modules?
en.displayIntent: Check practical understanding of the two Node.js module systems.
guidance: Name the syntax used by each system.
Explain how Node decides module mode.
Mention at least one practical interop issue.
commonMistakes: Reducing the difference to syntax only.
Ignoring package.json type.
Assuming every import direction works automatically.

vi.title: CommonJS và ES Modules
vi.displayQuestion: CommonJS và ES Modules khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết thực tế về hai hệ module trong Node.js.
guidance: Nêu cú pháp của từng hệ module.
Giải thích Node chọn module mode như thế nào.
Nhắc ít nhất một vấn đề interop thực tế.
commonMistakes: Chỉ xem khác biệt là cú pháp.
Bỏ qua package.json type.
Tưởng mọi chiều import đều tự hoạt động.

ja.title: CommonJS と ES Modules
ja.displayQuestion: CommonJS と ES Modules はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Node.js の 2 つの module system を実務的に理解しているか確認する。
guidance: それぞれの syntax を挙げる。
Node が module mode をどう決めるか説明する。
実際の interop 問題を少なくとも 1 つ述べる。
commonMistakes: 違いは syntax だけだと考える。
package.json type を無視する。
どの方向の import も自動的に動くと思う。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Modules - CommonJS
sourceReferences.note: Used for CommonJS behavior and require semantics.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: ECMAScript modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for ES module syntax, loading, and interoperability context.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Packages
sourceReferences.note: Used for package.json type and package boundary behavior.

intent: Verify that the candidate understands the concrete APIs used to consume and expose code in CommonJS and ES Modules.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between require/module.exports and import/export?
expectedSignals: Explains that require loads a module in CommonJS and returns the value assigned to module.exports.
Explains that module.exports is the value a CommonJS module exposes, while exports is only a shortcut when used carefully.
Explains that import/export are ES Module declarations for named exports, default exports, and importing bindings from another module.
Mentions that import declarations are normally static and placed at the top level, while dynamic import() exists for runtime loading.
Understands that projects should avoid mixing styles casually because transpilation and CommonJS/ESM interop can change the imported shape.
redFlags: Thinks module.exports and exports are always identical even after reassignment.
Cannot show a simple example of exporting and importing a function.
Says import works anywhere exactly like require without mentioning dynamic import.
Mixes default and named imports without understanding the exported shape.

scoringHints.description: Explains require return values, module.exports, exports shortcut caveat, ES named/default exports, and dynamic import at a practical level.
Can use both styles correctly but misses one nuance such as exports reassignment or dynamic import.
Has copied syntax before but cannot explain what value is being exported or imported.
Answer would likely produce incorrect import shapes in real code.

followUps.question: Can you write a tiny example that exports an add function and imports it in another file using either module system?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete syntax and exported-shape reasoning.

followUps.question: What is the problem with assigning exports = myFunction in CommonJS?
followUps.purpose: Check understanding of the exports shortcut.

en.title: require/module.exports and import/export
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between require/module.exports and import/export?
en.displayIntent: Check concrete module import and export mechanics.
guidance: Explain CommonJS require and module.exports.
Explain ES Module named and default exports.
Mention exported shape and interop pitfalls.
commonMistakes: Reassigning exports incorrectly.
Confusing default and named exports.
Using import as if it were exactly the same as require.

vi.title: require/module.exports và import/export
vi.displayQuestion: require/module.exports khác import/export như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cơ chế import và export module ở mức thực tế.
guidance: Giải thích require và module.exports trong CommonJS.
Giải thích named export và default export trong ES Module.
Nhắc tới exported shape và lỗi interop thường gặp.
commonMistakes: Gán lại exports sai cách.
Nhầm default export với named export.
Dùng import như thể giống hệt require.

ja.title: require/module.exports と import/export
ja.displayQuestion: require/module.exports と import/export はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: module の import/export mechanics を具体的に理解しているか確認する。
guidance: CommonJS の require と module.exports を説明する。
ES Module の named export と default export を説明する。
exported shape と interop の落とし穴に触れる。
commonMistakes: exports を誤って再代入する。
default export と named export を混同する。
import を require と完全に同じものとして使う。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Modules - CommonJS
sourceReferences.note: Used for require, module.exports, and exports shortcut behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: ECMAScript modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for import/export and ES module semantics.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands package.json as the project manifest used by npm and Node.js tooling.
expectedSignals: Describes package.json as the project manifest for a Node.js package or application.
Mentions dependency declarations such as dependencies and devDependencies.
Mentions scripts such as start, test, build, and dev that standardize common commands.
Mentions package metadata such as name, version, license, main, type, engines, or exports depending on the project.
Explains that npm and other tooling read package.json to install packages, run scripts, and understand package entry points.
redFlags: Says package.json stores installed package source code.
Does not know dependencies are declared there.
Thinks package.json is only needed when publishing to npm.
Suggests storing secrets or environment-specific credentials in package.json.

scoringHints.description: Explains manifest purpose, dependencies, scripts, metadata, entry points, and tooling integration.
Knows package.json contains dependencies and scripts but misses package metadata or Node entry point fields.
Has seen package.json but cannot explain how npm and Node tooling use it.
Answer misunderstands the file's role or suggests unsafe usage.

followUps.question: Which fields in package.json would you expect to check first when onboarding to a Node.js service?
followUps.purpose: Probe practical familiarity with project manifests.

followUps.question: What can the package.json type field change in a Node.js project?
followUps.purpose: Connect package metadata to module behavior.

en.title: package.json purpose
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands package.json as a Node.js project manifest.
guidance: Mention dependencies and scripts.
Mention metadata and entry point fields.
Explain how npm and tooling use the file.
commonMistakes: Thinking it stores dependency source code.
Thinking it is only for published libraries.
Putting secrets in package.json.

vi.title: Mục đích của package.json
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu package.json là manifest của project Node.js hay không.
guidance: Nhắc tới dependencies và scripts.
Nhắc tới metadata và entry point.
Giải thích npm và tooling dùng file này như thế nào.
commonMistakes: Tưởng file này chứa source code của dependency.
Tưởng chỉ library publish lên npm mới cần file này.
Đưa secret vào package.json.

ja.title: package.json の目的
ja.displayQuestion: package.json は何に使われますか。
ja.displayIntent: package.json を Node.js project manifest として理解しているか確認する。
guidance: dependencies と scripts に触れる。
metadata と entry point fields に触れる。
npm と tooling がこの file をどう使うか説明する。
commonMistakes: dependency source code を保存する file だと思う。
公開 library だけに必要だと思う。
package.json に secret を入れる。

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: package.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for package manifest fields, dependencies, scripts, and metadata.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: Packages
sourceReferences.note: Used for Node.js package entry points and package type behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate can distinguish runtime dependencies from development-only dependencies and understand deployment impact.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between dependencies and devDependencies?
expectedSignals: Explains that dependencies are packages needed by the application or library at runtime.
Explains that devDependencies are packages needed for development, testing, linting, formatting, building, or local tooling.
Gives practical examples such as express in dependencies and jest, eslint, typescript, or nodemon in devDependencies depending on the project.
Mentions npm install --save-dev or npm install -D for adding development dependencies.
Understands production installs may omit devDependencies, so putting runtime packages in devDependencies can break production, while putting dev tools in dependencies can bloat deploys.
redFlags: Says devDependencies are lower-quality or optional versions of the same packages.
Puts runtime server libraries in devDependencies without recognizing production risk.
Says the distinction never matters because npm always installs everything everywhere.
Cannot give any example of a development-only dependency.

scoringHints.description: Clearly distinguishes runtime and development dependency categories, gives examples, and explains production install consequences.
Understands the basic distinction but misses deployment or install-mode implications.
Can define the words but cannot apply them to real packages.
Answer would likely create broken or bloated production deployments.

followUps.question: Where would you put TypeScript in a project that compiles to JavaScript before deployment?
followUps.purpose: Probe build-time versus runtime dependency reasoning.

followUps.question: What can happen if express is placed only in devDependencies and the production install omits dev dependencies?

en.title: dependencies versus devDependencies
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between dependencies and devDependencies?
en.displayIntent: Check runtime versus development dependency understanding.
guidance: Define runtime dependencies.
Define development-only dependencies.
Explain production install impact.
commonMistakes: Putting runtime packages in devDependencies.
Assuming the distinction never matters.
Not knowing development-only examples.

vi.title: dependencies và devDependencies
vi.displayQuestion: dependencies và devDependencies khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về dependency runtime và dependency chỉ dùng khi phát triển.
guidance: Định nghĩa dependency cần ở runtime.
Định nghĩa dependency chỉ dùng cho development.
Giải thích ảnh hưởng khi install production.
commonMistakes: Đặt package runtime vào devDependencies.
Tưởng phân biệt này không bao giờ quan trọng.
Không nêu được ví dụ dependency chỉ dùng khi dev.

ja.title: dependencies と devDependencies
ja.displayQuestion: dependencies と devDependencies はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: runtime dependency と development-only dependency の理解を確認する。
guidance: runtime dependency を定義する。
development-only dependency を定義する。
production install への影響を説明する。
commonMistakes: runtime package を devDependencies に置く。
この区別は重要ではないと思う。
development-only dependency の例を挙げられない。

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: package.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for dependencies and devDependencies manifest fields.

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: npm install
sourceReferences.note: Used for install behavior and save-dev usage.

intent: Verify that the candidate understands package-lock.json as a reproducibility and dependency-tree control mechanism.
primaryQuestion: What is package-lock.json, and why should it be committed?
expectedSignals: Explains that package-lock.json records the exact resolved dependency tree, including transitive dependencies.
Mentions that it captures resolved versions, package sources, and integrity information used by npm.
Explains that committing it helps teammates, CI, and production builds install the same dependency versions.
Connects the lockfile to reliable npm ci installs and easier review of dependency changes.
Understands that package-lock.json is different from node_modules: the lockfile is committed, while installed package files usually are not.
redFlags: Says package-lock.json is useless because package.json already lists dependencies.
Says it should never be committed for applications.
Confuses package-lock.json with node_modules.
Deletes lockfile changes casually to make dependency conflicts disappear.

scoringHints.description: Explains exact dependency tree, transitive versions, integrity, reproducible installs, npm ci, and why it belongs in source control.
Knows it locks versions and should be committed but misses transitive dependencies or integrity detail.
Has seen lockfiles but cannot explain what problem they solve.
Answer would undermine reproducible builds or dependency review.

followUps.question: What should happen in CI if package.json and package-lock.json disagree?
followUps.purpose: Connect lockfile understanding to npm ci behavior.

followUps.question: Why is committing node_modules not the same as committing package-lock.json?
followUps.purpose: Separate installed artifacts from reproducible dependency metadata.

en.title: package-lock.json purpose
en.displayQuestion: What is package-lock.json, and why should it be committed?
en.displayIntent: Check lockfile and reproducible install understanding.
guidance: Explain exact resolved dependency tree.
Mention transitive dependencies and integrity.
Connect committing the file to CI and teammate consistency.
commonMistakes: Thinking package.json alone is enough for exact installs.
Confusing the lockfile with node_modules.
Deleting lockfile changes without understanding them.

vi.title: Mục đích của package-lock.json
vi.displayQuestion: package-lock.json là gì, và vì sao nên commit nó?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về lockfile và install có thể tái lập.
guidance: Giải thích cây dependency đã resolve chính xác.
Nhắc tới transitive dependency và integrity.
Liên hệ việc commit file với CI và sự nhất quán giữa các máy.
commonMistakes: Tưởng package.json đủ để install chính xác tuyệt đối.
Nhầm lockfile với node_modules.
Xóa thay đổi lockfile mà không hiểu.

ja.title: package-lock.json の目的
ja.displayQuestion: package-lock.json とは何で、なぜ commit すべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: lockfile と reproducible install の理解を確認する。
guidance: 正確に解決された dependency tree を説明する。
transitive dependency と integrity に触れる。
commit する理由を CI や team consistency と結びつける。
commonMistakes: package.json だけで完全に同じ install になると思う。
lockfile と node_modules を混同する。
lockfile の変更を理解せず削除する。

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: package-lock.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for package-lock purpose, dependency tree metadata, and reproducibility.

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: npm ci
sourceReferences.note: Used for lockfile-based clean installs.

intent: Check whether the candidate can choose between npm install and npm ci for local development versus repeatable CI builds.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between npm install and npm ci?
expectedSignals: Explains that npm install installs dependencies and may update package-lock.json when dependency resolution changes.
Explains that npm install is commonly used locally and when adding or changing packages.
Explains that npm ci performs a clean install based on package-lock.json and removes existing node_modules before installing.
Mentions that npm ci fails when package.json and package-lock.json are out of sync instead of silently rewriting the lockfile.
Identifies npm ci as the preferred command for CI or reproducible build environments.
redFlags: Says npm ci is just a faster alias for npm install.
Uses npm install in CI without recognizing possible lockfile drift.
Thinks npm ci can run without a lockfile.
Does not know that npm ci removes existing node_modules.

scoringHints.description: Contrasts local install and clean CI install, including lockfile requirements, mismatch failure, node_modules removal, and reproducibility.
Knows npm ci is for CI and uses the lockfile but misses one behavior such as removal or mismatch failure.
Has used both commands but cannot explain operational differences.
Answer would allow nondeterministic or broken CI dependency installs.

followUps.question: Why is npm ci usually safer than npm install in a CI pipeline?
What happens if package.json changed but package-lock.json was not updated before running npm ci?

en.title: npm install versus npm ci
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between npm install and npm ci?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands repeatable dependency installation.
guidance: Explain what npm install can change.
Explain npm ci clean install behavior.
Connect npm ci to CI reproducibility.
commonMistakes: Calling npm ci only a faster install.
Forgetting lockfile mismatch failures.
Not knowing node_modules is removed.

vi.title: npm install và npm ci
vi.displayQuestion: npm install và npm ci khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về install dependency có thể tái lập.
guidance: Giải thích npm install có thể thay đổi gì.
Giải thích behavior clean install của npm ci.
Liên hệ npm ci với tính ổn định trong CI.
commonMistakes: Gọi npm ci chỉ là npm install nhanh hơn.
Quên trường hợp package.json và lockfile lệch nhau.
Không biết node_modules bị xóa trước khi npm ci install.

ja.title: npm install と npm ci
ja.displayQuestion: npm install と npm ci はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: 再現可能な dependency installation を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: npm install が何を変更し得るか説明する。
npm ci の clean install behavior を説明する。
npm ci を CI reproducibility と結びつける。
commonMistakes: npm ci を速い npm install だと思う。
lockfile mismatch failure を忘れる。
node_modules が削除されることを知らない。

sourceReferences.note: Used for npm install behavior and dependency saving.

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: npm ci
sourceReferences.note: Used for clean install, lockfile requirements, and CI behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands npm scripts as standardized project commands defined in package.json.
expectedSignals: Explains that npm scripts are named commands defined under the scripts field in package.json.
Mentions common examples such as start, test, build, lint, and dev.
Explains that scripts can be run with npm run script-name, with some common shortcuts such as npm test or npm start.
Mentions that npm makes locally installed binaries from node_modules/.bin available to scripts, so teams can avoid relying on global tools.
Understands that scripts should be reviewed because they execute shell commands and can affect local, CI, or deployment environments.
redFlags: Thinks npm scripts are JavaScript functions exported by Node modules.
Cannot identify where scripts are defined.
Assumes scripts require tools to be installed globally.
Runs unknown scripts without recognizing they execute commands on the machine.

scoringHints.description: Explains scripts field, npm run usage, common commands, local binary resolution, and command-execution caution.
Knows how to run and define scripts but misses local binary PATH or safety implications.
Has run npm commands but cannot explain package.json scripts.
Answer misrepresents scripts or ignores their execution behavior.

followUps.question: Why can an npm script run eslint even if eslint is not installed globally?
How would you pass an extra argument through an npm script command?

en.title: npm scripts
en.displayIntent: Check practical understanding of package.json command scripts.
guidance: Define scripts as named package.json commands.
Mention npm run and common examples.
Explain local binary availability and command execution caution.
commonMistakes: Thinking scripts are exported JavaScript functions.
Not knowing scripts live in package.json.
Depending on global tool installs unnecessarily.

vi.title: npm scripts
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết thực tế về command scripts trong package.json.
guidance: Định nghĩa script là command có tên trong package.json.
Nhắc npm run và các ví dụ thường gặp.
Giải thích local binary và lưu ý script là command được execute.
commonMistakes: Tưởng script là JavaScript function được export.
Không biết script nằm trong package.json.
Phụ thuộc không cần thiết vào tool cài global.

ja.title: npm scripts
ja.displayQuestion: npm scripts とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: package.json command scripts の実務的な理解を確認する。
guidance: script を package.json 内の named command として定義する。
npm run と一般的な例に触れる。
local binary availability と command execution の注意点を説明する。
commonMistakes: script を export された JavaScript function だと思う。
script が package.json にあることを知らない。
不要に global tool install に依存する。

intent: Check whether the candidate understands node_modules as an installed dependency artifact and why teams usually avoid committing it.
primaryQuestion: What is node_modules, and why is it usually not committed?
expectedSignals: Explains that node_modules is the directory where npm installs project dependencies and their transitive dependencies.
Mentions that Node.js module resolution commonly looks in node_modules when resolving package imports.
Explains that node_modules is usually large, generated, and can contain many platform-specific or environment-specific files.
States that package.json plus package-lock.json should be committed so node_modules can be recreated with npm install or npm ci.
Mentions that committing node_modules causes noisy diffs, repository bloat, and stale dependency artifacts in most application workflows.
redFlags: Says node_modules must be committed so the app can run in production.
Does not know node_modules is generated by package installation.
Commits node_modules instead of understanding lockfile-based reproducible installs.
Thinks deleting node_modules permanently deletes dependency declarations from the project.

scoringHints.description: Explains installed dependency tree, module resolution, generated artifact status, lockfile-based recreation, and repository-bloat risks.
Knows node_modules is installed dependencies and should not be committed but misses resolution or lockfile details.
Has seen the folder but cannot explain how it relates to package.json and package-lock.json.
Answer would lead to poor source control hygiene or unreproducible installs.

followUps.question: If node_modules is deleted, what files let you recreate it reliably?
followUps.purpose: Check package manifest and lockfile relationship.

followUps.question: Why can committing node_modules make code review and repository size worse?

en.title: node_modules and source control
en.displayQuestion: What is node_modules, and why is it usually not committed?
en.displayIntent: Check understanding of installed dependency artifacts and lockfile-based recreation.
guidance: Explain what gets installed in node_modules.
Mention module resolution and generated artifacts.
Explain why package.json and package-lock.json are committed instead.
commonMistakes: Committing node_modules for normal application workflows.
Thinking deletion removes dependency declarations.
Ignoring repository bloat and platform-specific files.

vi.title: node_modules và source control
vi.displayQuestion: node_modules là gì, và vì sao thường không commit nó?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về artifact dependency đã install và cách tái tạo bằng lockfile.
guidance: Giải thích những gì được install trong node_modules.
Nhắc module resolution và artifact được generate.
Giải thích vì sao commit package.json và package-lock.json thay vào đó.
commonMistakes: Commit node_modules cho workflow app thông thường.
Tưởng xóa node_modules là xóa dependency declaration.
Bỏ qua repository bloat và file phụ thuộc platform.

ja.title: node_modules と source control
ja.displayQuestion: node_modules とは何で、なぜ通常 commit しないのですか。
ja.displayIntent: installed dependency artifact と lockfile-based recreation の理解を確認する。
guidance: node_modules に何が install されるか説明する。
module resolution と generated artifact に触れる。
代わりに package.json と package-lock.json を commit する理由を説明する。
commonMistakes: 通常の app workflow で node_modules を commit する。
削除すると dependency declaration も消えると思う。
repository bloat や platform-specific files を無視する。

intent: Check whether the candidate can explain semantic versioning and common npm range operators well enough to avoid accidental dependency upgrades.
primaryQuestion: What is semantic versioning, and what do ^ and ~ mean?
expectedSignals: Explains semantic versioning as MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.
States that major versions indicate breaking changes, minor versions add backward-compatible functionality, and patch versions fix backward-compatible bugs.
Explains that caret ranges such as ^1.2.3 generally allow compatible updates below the next major version, such as 1.x.x but not 2.0.0.
Explains that tilde ranges such as ~1.2.3 generally allow patch updates within the same minor version, such as 1.2.x but not 1.3.0.
Mentions that lockfiles pin the exact installed version even when package.json contains a range, and dependency updates should still be reviewed and tested.
redFlags: Says ^ and ~ are the same.
Thinks semver guarantees that upgrades never break anything in practice.
Cannot identify major, minor, and patch positions.
Ignores package-lock.json when discussing what version is actually installed.

scoringHints.description: Explains MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, ^ and ~ ranges, lockfile interaction, and the need to test dependency updates.
Correctly explains semver and basic ranges but misses lockfile or real-world trust limitations.
Has a rough idea of version numbers but cannot reason about allowed upgrades.
Answer would lead to unsafe or misunderstood dependency upgrades.

followUps.question: For ^2.3.4, what major version would npm avoid installing?
If package.json says ^1.2.3 but package-lock.json records 1.2.5, which version will npm ci install?

en.title: Semantic versioning ranges
en.displayQuestion: What is semantic versioning, and what do ^ and ~ mean?
en.displayIntent: Check semver and npm range operator understanding.
guidance: Explain MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.
Explain ^ and ~ with examples.
Mention lockfiles and testing dependency updates.
commonMistakes: Treating ^ and ~ as identical.
Assuming semver prevents all breakages.
Ignoring the exact locked version.

vi.title: Semantic versioning và range
vi.displayQuestion: Semantic versioning là gì, và ^ với ~ có nghĩa gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về semver và operator range trong npm.
guidance: Giải thích MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.
Giải thích ^ và ~ bằng ví dụ.
Nhắc lockfile và việc test khi update dependency.
commonMistakes: Xem ^ và ~ là giống nhau.
Tưởng semver đảm bảo không bao giờ vỡ.
Bỏ qua version chính xác trong lockfile.

ja.title: Semantic versioning ranges
ja.displayQuestion: semantic versioning とは何で、^ と ~ は何を意味しますか。
ja.displayIntent: semver と npm range operator の理解を確認する。
guidance: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH を説明する。
^ と ~ を例で説明する。
lockfile と dependency update testing に触れる。
commonMistakes: ^ と ~ を同じものとして扱う。
semver がすべての破壊を防ぐと思う。
lockfile の exact version を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: About semantic versioning
sourceReferences.note: Used for semantic versioning concepts and version range behavior.

sourceReferences.label: npm Docs: package.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for dependency version ranges in package manifests.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands local versus global npm installs and why project tooling should usually be local.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between a local and global npm package?
expectedSignals: Explains that a local package is installed into the project, usually under that project's node_modules, and recorded in package.json when saved.
Explains that a global package is installed into npm's global prefix and is usually used for command-line tools available across projects.
Mentions that application code normally resolves imports from local project dependencies, not arbitrary global packages.
Explains that local dev tools can be run through npm scripts or npx, helping every teammate and CI use the project-defined version.
Mentions risks of global packages such as version drift between machines, permission issues, and hidden environment dependencies.
redFlags: Says global packages are automatically available to require from any Node.js application.
Installs project runtime dependencies globally instead of declaring them in package.json.
Cannot explain why CI should not depend on a developer's globally installed tool.
Thinks local packages can only be used by one file rather than by the whole project.

scoringHints.description: Clearly distinguishes install location, package resolution, CLI usage, npm scripts or npx, and global version-drift risks.
Understands local project packages versus global CLI tools but misses resolution or CI consistency implications.
Knows npm has -g but cannot decide when to use it.
Answer would create undeclared dependencies or inconsistent builds.

followUps.question: Why is eslint usually better as a devDependency than as a required global install for the team?
If express is installed globally, can your API safely omit it from package.json?
followUps.purpose: Expose misunderstanding of runtime dependency declaration.

en.title: Local and global npm packages
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between a local and global npm package?
en.displayIntent: Check package install scope and reproducibility understanding.
guidance: Explain local project installs.
Explain global CLI installs.
Mention npm scripts or npx for project tool versions.
commonMistakes: Using global packages as undeclared runtime dependencies.
Assuming global packages are require-able everywhere.
Ignoring CI and teammate version drift.

vi.title: npm package local và global
vi.displayQuestion: npm package local và global khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về scope install package và tính tái lập của project.
guidance: Giải thích install local trong project.
Giải thích install global cho CLI.
Nhắc npm scripts hoặc npx để dùng version tool của project.
commonMistakes: Dùng package global như dependency runtime không khai báo.
Tưởng package global có thể require ở mọi project.
Bỏ qua lệch version giữa CI và máy developer.

ja.title: local npm package と global npm package
ja.displayQuestion: local npm package と global npm package はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: package install scope と reproducibility の理解を確認する。
guidance: local project install を説明する。
global CLI install を説明する。
project tool version には npm scripts や npx を使うことに触れる。
commonMistakes: global package を未宣言の runtime dependency として使う。
global package はどこでも require できると思う。
CI と developer machine の version drift を無視する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for local and global npm folder layout.

intent: Check whether the candidate can describe how to create and export a simple local module using either CommonJS or ES Modules.
primaryQuestion: How do you create and export your own module?
expectedSignals: Explains that a local module is usually a separate file that contains focused code such as a utility function, service, configuration helper, or class.
Shows a CommonJS approach such as assigning module.exports or properties on exports, then importing with require and a relative path.
Shows an ES Module approach such as export function, export const, or export default, then importing with import syntax.
Mentions that the project module system must match Node's configuration, such as package.json type or .cjs/.mjs extensions.
Understands that module code should avoid unnecessary side effects at import time, especially for reusable helpers.
redFlags: Cannot show any valid export/import pair.
Mixes CommonJS and ES Module syntax randomly in the same file without knowing the project mode.
Thinks every file is automatically exported to the rest of the project.
Builds modules with heavy side effects on import, such as starting servers or opening connections, without recognizing the risk.

scoringHints.description: Can describe or write both CommonJS and ES Module examples, mentions module mode, relative imports, and side-effect control.
Can correctly create one style of module but misses module mode or side-effect concerns.
Understands file separation but cannot produce working import/export syntax.
Answer does not show usable local module creation knowledge.

followUps.question: Can you export a function called formatUser from one file and import it in another file?
How would your answer change if the project uses package.json with type set to module?

en.title: Creating a local module
en.displayQuestion: How do you create and export your own module?
en.displayIntent: Check practical ability to create reusable local Node.js modules.
guidance: Describe a separate focused file.
Show CommonJS or ES Module export/import syntax.
Mention project module mode and avoiding import-time side effects.
commonMistakes: Assuming files export automatically.
Mixing module systems without awareness.
Creating heavy side effects during import.

vi.title: Tạo module local
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn tạo và export module của mình như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng tạo module Node.js local có thể tái sử dụng.
guidance: Mô tả một file riêng có trách nhiệm rõ ràng.
Nêu cú pháp export/import CommonJS hoặc ES Module.
Nhắc module mode của project và tránh side effect khi import.
commonMistakes: Tưởng file tự động export.
Trộn module system mà không biết mode đang dùng.
Tạo side effect nặng ngay lúc import.

ja.title: local module を作る
ja.displayQuestion: 自分の module をどう作成して export しますか。
ja.displayIntent: 再利用可能な local Node.js module を作れるか確認する。
guidance: 責務が明確な separate file として説明する。
CommonJS または ES Module の export/import syntax を示す。
project module mode と import-time side effects の回避に触れる。
commonMistakes: file が自動的に export されると思う。
module system を理解せず混在させる。
import 時に重い side effect を起こす。

sourceReferences.note: Used for local CommonJS modules, require, and module.exports.

sourceReferences.note: Used for ES Module import and export syntax in Node.js.

intent: Check whether the candidate can distinguish callback, synchronous, and promise-based filesystem APIs and understand their event loop impact.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between fs.readFile, fs.readFileSync, and fs/promises?
expectedSignals: Explains that fs.readFile is asynchronous and uses a callback with an error-first pattern when the read completes.
Explains that fs.readFileSync blocks the current JavaScript thread until the file read finishes or throws an error.
Explains that fs/promises provides promise-based filesystem APIs that work naturally with async/await and reject on errors.
Mentions that all three read the whole file into memory, so streams may be better for large files.
Connects synchronous filesystem calls to event loop blocking, especially inside request handlers, while asynchronous forms let the server continue handling other work.
redFlags: Says fs.readFileSync is faster and therefore better for APIs.
Does not mention error handling differences between callback, thrown errors, and promise rejection.
Thinks fs/promises is a different filesystem rather than a promise-based API.
Ignores memory impact when reading very large files.

scoringHints.description: Contrasts callback, sync, and promise APIs; covers error handling, event loop blocking, async/await usage, and whole-file memory behavior.
Knows sync versus async and promises but misses memory or error-handling nuance.
Can name the APIs but cannot choose safely in request-serving code.
Answer would likely block the event loop or mishandle filesystem errors.

followUps.question: How would you handle an error from each of these three APIs?
followUps.purpose: Probe callback, try/catch, and promise rejection handling.

followUps.question: If the file can be hundreds of megabytes, what would you consider instead of readFile?

en.title: Node.js filesystem read APIs
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between fs.readFile, fs.readFileSync, and fs/promises?
en.displayIntent: Check callback, synchronous, and promise-based filesystem API understanding.
guidance: Explain callback-based fs.readFile.
Explain blocking behavior of fs.readFileSync.
Explain fs/promises with async/await and error handling.
Mention whole-file memory usage.
commonMistakes: Using sync reads inside request handlers.
Ignoring error handling differences.
Reading huge files fully into memory by default.

vi.title: API đọc file trong Node.js
vi.displayQuestion: fs.readFile, fs.readFileSync và fs/promises khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về API filesystem dạng callback, đồng bộ và promise.
guidance: Giải thích fs.readFile dùng callback.
Giải thích fs.readFileSync block event loop.
Giải thích fs/promises với async/await và xử lý lỗi.
Nhắc việc đọc toàn bộ file vào memory.
commonMistakes: Dùng sync read trong request handler.
Bỏ qua khác biệt xử lý lỗi.
Mặc định đọc file rất lớn vào memory.

ja.title: Node.js filesystem read APIs
ja.displayQuestion: fs.readFile、fs.readFileSync、fs/promises はどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: callback、synchronous、promise-based filesystem API の理解を確認する。
guidance: callback-based fs.readFile を説明する。
fs.readFileSync の blocking behavior を説明する。
fs/promises と async/await、error handling を説明する。
whole-file memory usage に触れる。
commonMistakes: request handler 内で sync read を使う。
error handling の違いを無視する。
巨大 file をデフォルトで全部 memory に読む。

sourceReferences.note: Used for callback, synchronous, and promise-based filesystem APIs.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands Buffer as Node.js binary data and can recognize where it appears in backend work.
primaryQuestion: What is a Buffer, and when might you see it in Node.js?
expectedSignals: Defines Buffer as a Node.js object for handling raw binary bytes outside normal JavaScript string semantics.
Mentions common places Buffers appear, such as file reads without an encoding, network data, uploads, streams, crypto hashes, and base64 conversion.
Explains that converting between Buffer and string requires an encoding such as utf8, hex, or base64.
Understands that string length and byte length can differ for non-ASCII text, so byte handling matters for protocols and storage limits.
Mentions safe allocation practices at a basic level, such as preferring Buffer.alloc when initialized zero-filled memory matters.
redFlags: Says Buffer is just an array of characters.
Converts binary data to string without considering encoding.
Confuses base64 encoding with encryption.
Ignores memory implications when buffering large uploads or files.

scoringHints.description: Explains binary bytes, common backend appearances, encoding conversion, byte length versus string length, and basic safe allocation awareness.
Understands Buffer as binary data and names use cases but misses encoding or memory nuance.
Has seen Buffer output but cannot explain what it represents.
Answer would likely corrupt binary data or mishandle encodings.

followUps.question: What happens if fs.readFile reads a file without an encoding option?
followUps.purpose: Check practical Buffer appearance in filesystem code.

followUps.question: Why can Buffer.byteLength('é', 'utf8') differ from 'é'.length?

en.title: Buffer and binary data
en.displayQuestion: What is a Buffer, and when might you see it in Node.js?
en.displayIntent: Check binary data and encoding fundamentals in Node.js.
guidance: Define Buffer as raw bytes.
Name filesystem, streams, uploads, crypto, and base64 examples.
Explain encoding conversion and byte length issues.
commonMistakes: Treating Buffer as a character array.
Ignoring encodings.
Confusing base64 with encryption.

vi.title: Buffer và dữ liệu nhị phân
vi.displayQuestion: Buffer là gì, và khi nào bạn có thể gặp nó trong Node.js?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng về binary data và encoding trong Node.js.
guidance: Định nghĩa Buffer là raw bytes.
Nêu ví dụ filesystem, stream, upload, crypto và base64.
Giải thích chuyển đổi encoding và vấn đề byte length.
commonMistakes: Xem Buffer là mảng ký tự.
Bỏ qua encoding.
Nhầm base64 với encryption.

ja.title: Buffer と binary data
ja.displayQuestion: Buffer とは何で、Node.js ではどんな場面で見ますか。
ja.displayIntent: Node.js の binary data と encoding の基礎を確認する。
guidance: Buffer を raw bytes として定義する。
filesystem、streams、uploads、crypto、base64 の例を挙げる。
encoding conversion と byte length の問題を説明する。
commonMistakes: Buffer を文字配列として扱う。
encoding を無視する。
base64 と encryption を混同する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for Buffer, encodings, byte length, and allocation behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: File system
sourceReferences.note: Used for filesystem APIs that may return Buffer objects.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands why synchronous filesystem work in request handlers can harm Node.js server responsiveness.
primaryQuestion: Why should you avoid synchronous filesystem calls inside API request handlers?
expectedSignals: Explains that synchronous filesystem calls block the Node.js event loop while the operation runs.
Connects event loop blocking to worse latency and throughput because other requests cannot be processed by the JavaScript thread during that time.
Mentions that a single slow disk operation or large file read can affect unrelated users, not just the current request.
Suggests alternatives such as fs/promises, callback-based fs APIs, streaming, caching data at startup, moving work outside the request path, or offloading heavy work.
Recognizes exceptions and nuance: small synchronous reads during startup may be acceptable, but per-request synchronous I/O in a server is risky.
redFlags: Says synchronous filesystem calls are fine because Node.js is fast.
Only considers the current request and ignores impact on other concurrent requests.
Replaces sync read with async read but still performs huge CPU-heavy parsing in the request path without considering blocking.
Cannot explain what the event loop has to do with request handling.

scoringHints.description: Explains event loop blocking, latency and throughput impact, cross-request effects, safer alternatives, and startup-versus-request nuance.
Knows sync calls block Node.js and suggests async alternatives but misses broader concurrency or parsing concerns.
Repeats that async is better but cannot explain the server impact.
Answer would allow blocking request handlers in production APIs.

followUps.question: If every request calls fs.readFileSync on a large JSON file, what happens under concurrent traffic?
When might a synchronous filesystem call be acceptable in a Node.js service?
followUps.purpose: Check practical nuance instead of rote avoidance.

en.title: Synchronous filesystem calls in APIs
en.displayQuestion: Why should you avoid synchronous filesystem calls inside API request handlers?
en.displayIntent: Check event loop and request-concurrency impact understanding.
guidance: Explain event loop blocking.
Connect blocking to latency and throughput.
Suggest async, streaming, caching, or moving work out of the request path.
Mention startup-time exceptions.
commonMistakes: Thinking sync I/O only affects one request.
Ignoring CPU parsing after async reads.
Treating all sync calls as equally acceptable in servers.

vi.title: Filesystem sync trong API
vi.displayQuestion: Vì sao nên tránh synchronous filesystem calls bên trong API request handler?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về event loop và ảnh hưởng tới concurrent request.
guidance: Giải thích event loop bị block.
Liên hệ blocking với latency và throughput.
Đề xuất async, streaming, cache hoặc chuyển việc ra khỏi request path.
Nhắc ngoại lệ khi chạy lúc startup.
commonMistakes: Tưởng sync I/O chỉ ảnh hưởng một request.
Bỏ qua CPU parsing sau khi đọc async.
Xem mọi sync call đều ổn trong server.

ja.title: API 内の synchronous filesystem calls
ja.displayQuestion: API request handler の中で synchronous filesystem calls を避けるべきなのはなぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: event loop と request concurrency への影響を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: event loop blocking を説明する。
blocking を latency と throughput に結びつける。
async、streaming、caching、request path 外への移動を提案する。
startup-time exception に触れる。
commonMistakes: sync I/O は 1 request だけに影響すると思う。
async read 後の CPU parsing を無視する。
server での sync call をすべて問題ないと考える。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js Learn: Don't Block the Event Loop
sourceReferences.note: Used for event loop blocking, worker pool, and server responsiveness guidance.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js API: File system
sourceReferences.note: Used for synchronous and asynchronous filesystem API behavior.
